claims.claim: We evaluate DFlash speculative decoding integrated into the vLLM serving framework on an NVIDIA GB10 GPU (SM121).

evidence_refs.quote: # DFlash Speculative Decoding in vLLM: Throughput and Quality on NVIDIA GB10

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Using Qwen3-4B as the target model and a DFlash-trained draft model (Qwen3-4B-DFlash-b16, 16 speculative tokens), we compare baseline autoregressive serving against DFlash-augmented serving across four prompt sets (chat, GSM8K, MATH-500, MBPP-local) at concurrency 4 with 256-token generation.

evidence_refs.quote: # DFlash Speculative Decoding in vLLM: Throughput and Quality on NVIDIA GB10

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: DFlash achieves throughput speedups of 1.61× (chat), 3.04× (GSM8K), 4.17× (MATH-500), and 3.96× (MBPP-local) with zero measured quality degradation and zero request failures in both modes.

evidence_refs.quote: # DFlash Speculative Decoding in vLLM: Throughput and Quality on NVIDIA GB10

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: A mini-matrix at concurrencies 1/4/8 with 128-token generation confirms the speedup across load levels, though the magnitude decreases with rising concurrency.

claims.claim: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive language model inference by using a smaller draft model to propose candidate token sequences that the target model verifies in parallel.

evidence_refs.quote: # DFlash Speculative Decoding in vLLM: Throughput and Quality on NVIDIA GB10

claims.claim: When the draft model's proposals are accepted, multiple tokens are produced per forward pass without modifying the target model's output distribution.

claims.claim: The practical impact of speculative decoding on serving throughput depends on the interaction between acceptance rate, batch composition, and the serving framework's scheduling logic.

claims.claim: Published DFlash results focus on per-request latency; the effect on aggregate serving throughput under concurrent load is less well characterized.
